static_findings.description:
TimeLockVesting.batchRelease(address) (../../../../../tmp/sentinel_static_a_edyqkg.sol#67-85) sends eth to arbitrary user
	Dangerous calls:
	- (ok,None) = user.call{value: releasable}() (../../../../../tmp/sentinel_static_a_edyqkg.sol#78)

Low level call in TimeLockVesting.release(uint256) (../../../../../tmp/sentinel_static_a_edyqkg.sol#47-65):
	- (ok,None) = s.beneficiary.call{value: releasable}() (../../../../../tmp/sentinel_static_a_edyqkg.sol#61)

Low level call in TimeLockVesting.revoke(uint256) (../../../../../tmp/sentinel_static_a_edyqkg.sol#87-99):
	- (ok,None) = owner.call{value: remaining}() (../../../../../tmp/sentinel_static_a_edyqkg.sol#96)

Low level call in TimeLockVesting.batchRelease(address) (../../../../../tmp/sentinel_static_a_edyqkg.sol#67-85):
	- (ok,None) = user.call{value: releasable}() (../../../../../tmp/sentinel_static_a_edyqkg.sol#78)

Reentrancy in TimeLockVesting.batchRelease(address) (../../../../../tmp/sentinel_static_a_edyqkg.sol#67-85):
	External calls:
	- (ok,None) = user.call{value: releasable}() (../../../../../tmp/sentinel_static_a_edyqkg.sol#78)
	State variables written after the call(s):
	- s.withdrawn += releasable (../../../../../tmp/sentinel_static_a_edyqkg.sol#77)
	TimeLockVesting.schedules (../../../../../tmp/sentinel_static_a_edyqkg.sol#20) can be used in cross function reentrancies:
	- TimeLockVesting.batchRelease(address) (../../../../../tmp/sentinel_static_a_edyqkg.sol#67-85)
	- TimeLockVesting.createBatchSchedules(address[],uint256[],uint256,bool) (../../../../../tmp/sentinel_static_a_edyqkg.sol#101-110)
	- TimeLockVesting.createSchedule(address,uint256,uint256,bool) (../../../../../tmp/sentinel_static_a_edyqkg.sol#36-45)
	- TimeLockVesting.release(uint256) (../../../../../tmp/sentinel_static_a_edyqkg.sol#47-65)
	- TimeLockVesting.revoke(uint256) (../../../../../tmp/sentinel_static_a_edyqkg.sol#87-99)
	- TimeLockVesting.schedules (../../../../../tmp/sentinel_static_a_edyqkg.sol#20)

Reentrancy in TimeLockVesting.release(uint256) (../../../../../tmp/sentinel_static_a_edyqkg.sol#47-65):
	External calls:
	- (ok,None) = s.beneficiary.call{value: releasable}() (../../../../../tmp/sentinel_static_a_edyqkg.sol#61)
	Event emitted after the call(s):
	- TokensReleased(scheduleId,releasable) (../../../../../tmp/sentinel_static_a_edyqkg.sol#63)

Reentrancy in TimeLockVesting.batchRelease(address) (../../../../../tmp/sentinel_static_a_edyqkg.sol#67-85):
	External calls:
	- (ok,None) = user.call{value: releasable}() (../../../../../tmp/sentinel_static_a_edyqkg.sol#78)
	Event emitted after the call(s):
	- TokensReleased(ids[i],releasable) (../../../../../tmp/sentinel_static_a_edyqkg.sol#80)

TimeLockVesting.batchRelease(address) (../../../../../tmp/sentinel_static_a_edyqkg.sol#67-85) uses timestamp for comparisons
	Dangerous comparisons:
	- elapsed > 0 (../../../../../tmp/sentinel_static_a_edyqkg.sol#73)
	- releasable > 0 (../../../../../tmp/sentinel_static_a_edyqkg.sol#76)
	- require(bool,string)(ok,batch release failed) (../../../../../tmp/sentinel_static_a_edyqkg.sol#79)

TimeLockVesting.release(uint256) (../../../../../tmp/sentinel_static_a_edyqkg.sol#47-65) uses timestamp for comparisons
	Dangerous comparisons:
	- require(bool,string)(msg.sender == s.beneficiary || msg.sender == owner,not authorized) (../../../../../tmp/sentinel_static_a_edyqkg.sol#49)
	- elapsed >= s.duration (../../../../../tmp/sentinel_static_a_edyqkg.sol#53)
	- require(bool,string)(releasable > 0,nothing to release) (../../../../../tmp/sentinel_static_a_edyqkg.sol#59)
	- require(bool,string)(ok,transfer failed) (../../../../../tmp/sentinel_static_a_edyqkg.sol#62)

TimeLockVesting.revoke(uint256) (../../../../../tmp/sentinel_static_a_edyqkg.sol#87-99) uses timestamp for comparisons
	Dangerous comparisons:
	- require(bool,string)(s.revocable,not revocable) (../../../../../tmp/sentinel_static_a_edyqkg.sol#89)
Consider introducing checks for `msg.sender` to ensure the recipient of the money is as intended.
Changing state after an external call can lead to re-entrancy attacks.Use the checks-effects-interactions pattern to avoid this issue.
Contracts have owners with privileged rights to perform admin tasks and need to be trusted to not perform malicious updates or drain funds.
Invoking `SSTORE` operations in loops may waste gas. Use a local variable to hold the loop computation result.

Solc compiler version 0.8.20 switches the default target EVM version to Shanghai, which means that the generated bytecode will include PUSH0 opcodes. Be sure to select the appropriate EVM version in case you intend to deploy on a chain other than mainnet like L2 chains that may not support PUSH0, otherwise deployment of your contracts will fail.

Avoid `require` / `revert` statements in a loop because a single bad item can cause the whole transaction to fail. It's better to forgive on fail and return failed elements post processing of the loop
There are state variable changes in this function but no event is emitted. Consider emitting an event to enable offchain indexers to track the changes.
State variables that are only changed in the constructor should be declared immutable to save gas. Add the `immutable` attribute to state variables that are only changed in the constructor
Consider using a specific version of Solidity in your contracts instead of a wide version. For example, instead of `pragma solidity ^0.8.0;`, use `pragma solidity 0.8.0;`

rag_evidence.content:
DeFi Security Incident: NST
Date: 2023-06-01
Vulnerability type: other

Attack summary:
Milktech is a software company that explores Polygon web3 technologies and recently ventured into
tokens and token payments. They created a token called NST, which maintains a constant price based
on USD. Several contracts were developed, with the main one being the swap contract. This contract
facilitates a straightforward exchange between two tokens: NST (the internal company token) and USDT,
ensuring a consistent price ratio. NST is an ERC-20 token with an additional role called the Minter,
allowing specific addresses to mint new tokens. Only the owner of the contract can assign this
role. The swap contract is ownable and features two primary functions: buyNST, which takes USDT as input,
and sellNST, which takes NST as input. Additionally, the contract includes the ability to pause trading
between the tokens. While the token itself was verified, the swap contract was not.

DeFi Security Incident: Phoenix
Date: 2023-03-01
Vulnerability type: access_control

Attack summary:
PhxProxy contract delegateCallSwap() function lack of access control and can be passed in any parameter
The lost money is mainly USDC in the d028 contract which the attacker converts into WETH in the 65ba contract through the buyLeverage function
and then swaps it into his own tokens by the delegateCallSwap function, making a profit from it

Post-mortem references:
https://twitter.com/HypernativeLabs/status/1633090456157401088

DeFi Security Incident: MARS
Date: 2024-04-01
Vulnerability type: other

Detailed notes:
Total Lost: >$100k
Attacker: 0x306174b707ebf6d7301a0bcd898ae1666ec176ae
Attack Contract: 0x797acb321cb10154aa807fcd1e155c34135483cd
Attack Contract: 0x797acb321cb10154aa807fcd1e155c34135483cd
Vulnerable Contract: 0x3dC7E6FF0fB79770FA6FB05d1ea4deACCe823943
Attack Tx: https://app.blocksec.com/explorer/tx/bsc/0x25e2af0a55581d5629a933af9fedd3c70e6d0c320f0b72700ca80e5cdd36c80b
@Analyses
https://twitter.com/Phalcon_xyz/status/1780150315603701933
The pair contract can get reflections from taxes. Thus the attacker can user flashloan to repeated swap and sync for better pricing.
emit log_named_uint("get MARS", MARS.balanceOf(address(receiver)));
emit log_named_uint("get MARS", MARS.balanceOf(address(receiver)));

final_report.recommendation:
HIGH RISK — top vulnerability: Timestamp (probability 89.8%, CONFIRMED tier). 5 similar exploit pattern(s) found in DeFiHackLabs corpus. 4 Slither High/Medium finding(s). 2 prior on-chain audit(s). Recommend full manual audit before deployment.

> **NOTE:** Contract exceeded 512 CodeBERT tokens — tail code was not analysed. Manual review of the unanalysed portion is recommended.

unused_evidence: 2 High/Medium static finding(s) not tied to a verdict: eth-send-unchecked-address, reentrancy-state-change
failure_modes: Contract exceeded 512 CodeBERT tokens — tail code unanalysed; verdicts may miss vulnerabilities in the truncated region.
ExternalBug flagged — the ML model is known to over-predict this class (Run 12 class-definition mismatch); treat as lower confidence unless corroborated by an inter-contract call finding.
reflection_notes.summary: Self-critique: 5 verdict(s) are uncertain; 1 evidence item(s) unused; 2 failure mode(s) noted.
